front: Work: the master-slave dialectic describes two self-conscious beings recognizing each other through a 'struggle to the death'; the bondsman rediscovers himself through labor
work: The Phenomenology of Spirit and the Dialectic

front: Philosopher: Susan Buck-Morss argues the master-slave dialectic was influenced by accounts of the Haitian Revolution in journals like Minerva
work: The Phenomenology of Spirit and the Dialectic

front: Philosopher: Aufhebung (sublation) resolves contradictions of Being and Nothing into Becoming in the dialectical process
work: The Phenomenology of Spirit and the Dialectic

front: Philosopher: Alexandre Kojeve's influential reading of this thinker shaped Fukuyama's End of History; argued USA and USSR represented two interpretations
work: The Phenomenology of Spirit and the Dialectic

front: Work: develops Sittlichkeit (ethical life) through three spheres: abstract right, morality, and ethical life (family, civil society, state)
back: Elements of the Philosophy of Right (Hegel)
work: Elements of the Philosophy of Right and Ethical Life

front: Philosopher: philosophy arrives too late to make a difference, like 'the owl of Minerva [that] spreads its wings only with the falling of the dusk'
work: Elements of the Philosophy of Right and Ethical Life

front: Philosopher: described history as spirit discovering freedom through 'world-historical figures' like Napoleon; coined the 'cunning of reason'
Philosopher: Lectures on the Philosophy of History infamously claim Africa 'has no history' and divide history into original, reflective, and philosophical approaches
Philosopher: freedom progresses from Orientals (one free), to Greeks (some free), to modern men (all free)
Work: sections on Being, Essence, and Concept; calls logic a 'realm of shadows'; rejects the law of non-contradiction
Philosopher: aesthetics distinguish classical, romantic, and symbolic art; argue art 'in its highest vocation remains for us a thing of the past'
Philosopher: Arthur Danto modernized this thinker's 'end of art' thesis via Andy Warhol's Brillo Boxes
Philosopher: after death in 1831, followers split into Right/Old Hegelians (Lutheran church) and Left/Young Hegelians (Feuerbach, Marx, Stirner)
Philosopher: David Friedrich Strauss coined the terms 'Left Hegelian' and 'Right Hegelian' while defending The Life of Jesus
Philosopher: Kierkegaard attacked 'the system,' replacing 'the truth is the whole' with truth as subjectivity in Concluding Unscientific Postscript
Philosopher: the Pittsburgh school (McDowell's Mind and World, Brandom's Making It Explicit) revives this thinker's ideas
